name: Instagram: Search
description: Open Instagram, type a query into the nav search rail, and return the visible result rows. Use mode to filter the rows the workflow returns: accounts (handle profile rows), hashtags (/explore/tags/ rows), places (/explore/locations/ rows), or top (all rows in the order Instagram surfaces them). Use this when you want to resolve a handle, hashtag, or place from a partial string without opening individual profiles. Do not use this to open a known handle directly — navigate to https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/ instead.

help.summary: Search Instagram from the nav rail and return accounts, hashtags, or places matching a query.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search string. Typed character-by-character into the nav search input, matching how a human would drive the typeahead.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: top returns every result row in Instagram's native order (accounts, tags, places, mixed). accounts returns only /handle/ profile rows. hashtags returns only /explore/tags/ rows. places returns only /explore/locations/ rows. Defaults to top.

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of result rows to return after mode filtering. Defaults to 20. Cap is 50.

examples.description: Find the top results for a partial query
Return only account rows for a handle fragment
examples.command: rzn-browser run instagram search --param query="portland" --param mode="accounts" --param max_results="5"
rzn-browser run instagram search --param query="rctid" --param mode="hashtags"

help.returns: query (echoed), mode (echoed), input_accepted, total_results, results [{kind (account|hashtag|place|other), handle (accounts only), tag (hashtags only), place_slug (places only), display_name, full_text, result_url, position}].
notes: Requires a logged-in Instagram session in the active Chrome profile; returns results_ready=false if the signed-out home fails to render the search rail.
Opens a dedicated workflow tab (use_current_tab=false), so this workflow is safe to run in parallel with other dedicated-tab instagram flows.

Instagram only renders the category tab (Accounts / Tags / Places) for a category that actually has matches, so mode="hashtags" on a handle-only query returns an empty results array rather than an error.
Rows are deduped by href because Instagram renders each result with both an image link and a text link pointing at the same target.

browser_automation.description: Opens a dedicated workflow tab, navigates to the Instagram home, clicks the search nav entry, types the query with real keystrokes, waits for typeahead results, and returns the deduped result rows filtered by mode.

sequences.name: instagram_search
sequences.description: Drive the Instagram nav search rail and return filtered result rows.

steps.name: Open the search rail, type the query, collect results
